text: Fill in the blanks on Mouth, Pharynx and Palate anatomy
questions: The parotid duct (Stensen's duct) opens into the vestibule opposite the upper *second/2nd* molar tooth.
General sensation to the anterior two-thirds of the tongue is supplied by the *lingual* nerve, a branch of CN V3.
Taste from the anterior two-thirds of the tongue is carried by the *chorda tympani* nerve, a branch of CN VII.
The *glossopharyngeal* nerve (CN IX) supplies both general sensation and taste to the posterior one-third of the tongue.
All intrinsic and extrinsic muscles of the tongue are supplied by the *hypoglossal* nerve (CN XII), except palatoglossus which is supplied by CN X.
The primary protrusor of the tongue is the *genioglossus* muscle.
The ring of lymphoid tissue at the pharyngeal entrance is called *Waldeyer's/Waldeyer* lymphatic ring.
Killian's dehiscence is the weak area between the two parts of the *inferior constrictor/inferior pharyngeal constrictor* muscle where Zenker's diverticulum herniates.
The soft palate contains *five/5* muscles that control its movement during swallowing and speech.
The motor supply to the pharyngeal constrictors is provided by the *vagus* nerve (CN X) via the pharyngeal plexus.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
